title: Robust Russian Black Bean Pilaf
description: A hearty, one-pot Russian dish featuring black beans, with a nutty and crunchy texture, made with a mortar and pestle and cooked on a salt block, perfect for a low-carb diet
instructions: Heat the salt block over medium heat for 10 minutes
Pound the caraway seeds and garlic in a mortar and pestle until well combined
Add the chopped onion to the mortar and pestle and pound until the onion is translucent
Add the soaked black beans, pumpkin seeds, salt, and black pepper to the mortar and pestle and mix until well combined
Add the black bean mixture to the heated salt block and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally
Add the chicken broth to the salt block and bring to a boil
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed and the beans are tender
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
